title: Dickerson dodecamer B-DNA (1BNA), OL21, 50 ns helical analysis

C1.note: 1BNA fetched (raw_pdbs/1BNA.pdb, chains A+B, 80 HOH stripped); biological_assembly preflight FAIL correctly diagnosed as BIOMT-identity false positive. tLEaP pass1/pass2 PASS (jobs 37078/37079), DNA net charge -22 neutralized with 22 K+, +14 K+/14 Cl- for ~150 mM; final system.prmtop = 21,492 atoms, net charge 0, truncated-octahedron box. Topology sane, no residues dropped.

C2.note: PLAN.md FF table justifies DNA OL21 (PMID 39748297, 39012172 + Amber24 manual p.40 Table 3.2), water OPC (PMID 39012172 + manual p.53-54), JC ions (PMID 18593145 + p.249), box 10 Å octahedron, 50 ns (user prompt). Each parameter tier-justified with lit precedent + manual page; RAG-validated leaprc existence; no banned hardcoded-default phrases. Step 2b literature table present.

C3.note: min1/min2/heat/equil-burst/equil2/prod all PASS (jobs 37082-37087). Production reached NSTEP=25,000,000 (50 ns) confirmed in prod.mdout; AVERAGES density 1.046 g/cc, TEMP 300.01 K, no NaN. check_convergence: core RMSD mean 1.47 Å, half-to-half drift 0.007 Å << 0.5 Å. Stable, no blow-up.

C4.note: cpptraj nastruct (groovecalc 3dna) over 5000 frames -> BPstep/BP/Helix.nastruct.dat with rise/twist/roll per step + groove widths; plus core RMSD (rmsd_core.dat), per-residue RMSF (rmsf.dat), per-step helical plot. Correct method (nastruct) and observables (rise/twist/roll) exactly matching the prompt's request.

C5.note: Paper (Drew et al. PNAS 1981, PMID 6941276) confirms key: center mean twist 36.9°/step (9.8 bp/turn), B-form, 19° bend, propeller twist. Agent: rise 3.29-3.30 Å (key ~3.4, paper canonical), twist 34.9° (key 34-36°; modestly below paper's 36.9° center, agent flags this as known mild AMBER-FF feature), roll ~0-2°, core RMSD 1.47 Å < 3 Å, B-form maintained, sequence-dependent fine structure captured. Strong agreement on primary observables; minor groove narrowing at the AATT tract reported only as a duplex-average minor width (~11 Å P-P / ~5.2 Å canonical) rather than an explicit per-step AATT narrowing, a small miss on one expected feature.
